activities_projects.title: Reading India’s Population Pyramid
activities_projects.objective: Introduce students to the idea of age–sex composition using a simple population pyramid.
materials: Printed population pyramid of India (latest census or textbook figure)
steps: Observe the given population pyramid and identify the three broad groups: young (0–14), working-age (15–59), and aged (60+).
Shade each age group in a different colour on the pyramid.
Estimate which group is the largest and write 3–4 lines on what this means for schools, jobs, and healthcare.
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Understands basic age–sex composition and can interpret a simple population pyramid.
activities_projects.evaluation: Correct identification of age groups and a short written interpretation (10 marks).
